summary: Tested admin user management: GET /api/admin/users, PUT role/ban endpoints, banned user login block, frontend Pengguna tab with user table, search, dropdown actions. 12/13 backend tests pass.

minor.issue: Users who have never been banned don't have 'banned' field in response (missing default). Frontend uses .get('banned') so works, but response is inconsistent. Fix: add 'banned': u.get('banned', False) in the list comprehension.

action_items: Fix GET /api/admin/users to include 'banned: false' default for users who haven't been banned: add u['banned'] = u.get('banned', False) in the loop at line 627-628
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/admin/users missing default 'banned: false' for users without a banned field set - causes inconsistent API responses
context_for_next_testing_agent: User management fully functional. Tabs (Produk/Pengguna) work. User table shows name, email, role badge, status (Aktif/Nonaktif), join date. Search filters correctly. (Anda) label shown on admin row, no action menu for self. Dropdown shows 'Jadikan Admin'/'Nonaktifkan' options. Role change, ban/unban all work. Banned user login returns 403. Only minor: banned field missing default in GET response.
rca_of_issue: MongoDB documents for admin/original users created before the 'banned' field was added don't have the field. The GET endpoint returns raw documents without setting a default. Fix: in admin_get_users, add u['banned'] = u.get('banned', False) before returning.
